claim: Always serialize timestamps in UTC to avoid cross-region comparison bugs
rationale: prior incidents with mixed local/UTC timestamps in order ledger

claim: Next.js route group folders like (protected) are filesystem-only and never appear in browser URLs. The isSellerRoute middleware matcher must use the actual URL paths (/seller/dashboard, /seller/edit, /seller/upload) — never the folder names.
rationale: proxy.ts had '/(protected)/seller(.*)' which never matched any real URL. /seller/edit and /seller/upload were silently unprotected — currentUser() returned null and triggered redirect('/') instead of loading the page. Pain score: 8.

claim: generateMetadata() in Next.js Server Components must never throw — wrap in try/catch and return a fallback. An uncaught throw inside generateMetadata surfaces as a cryptic unrelated error (e.g. ClerkAPIResponseError) at the page level.
rationale: EditProductPage crashed with 'ClerkAPIResponseError' because generateMetadata called getProductById which threw on Supabase errors. The real error was hidden. Fixed by wrapping in try/catch and making getProductById return null instead of throwing.

claim: Service methods (e.g. getProductById) that are called from both page components AND generateMetadata must return null on all error paths — never throw. Throwing from a service causes the entire Server Component tree to fail with misleading errors.
rationale: ProductService.getProductById threw on non-404 Supabase errors. This caused generateMetadata (which called it) to crash the page render with a fake ClerkAPIResponseError. Consistent return-null pattern prevents this category of bug.

claim: Browser <input type='file'> cannot be pre-populated for security reasons — it always shows 'No file chosen'. In edit forms with existing files, use a hidden sr-only input + a custom Button that triggers it via ref.current.click(), and show the current filename in a controlled span alongside it.
rationale: Seller edit form showed 'No file chosen' for both cover image and product file even though files already existed in Supabase. Fixed by replacing native file input UI with a custom button + controlled state span pattern.

claim: When new DB columns are added to existing tables (e.g. file_size, file_extension in Phase 2F), existing rows will have NULL. Always provide a SQL backfill script AND a display-layer fallback (e.g. derive file_extension from the file_url path). Don't rely on column data being present for pre-migration rows.
rationale: Phase 2F added file_size and file_extension columns. All existing products showed N/A on the product detail page. Fixed by (a) deriving extension from file_url path in the UI and (b) providing a SQL backfill for the extension column.
